input.title: Corporate Expense Management
input.description: Corporate Expense Management is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Plan Employee Registration and Onboarding Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for employee registration and onboarding and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Implement complete employee registration and onboarding functionality; All employee registration and onboarding operations must be validated and authorized; System shall maintain audit log for employee registration and onboarding actions.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for employee registration and onboarding is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for employee registration and onboarding are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for employee registration and onboarding can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Employee Registration and Onboarding Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for employee registration and onboarding on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Implement complete employee registration and onboarding functionality; All employee registration and onboarding operations must be validated and authorized; System shall maintain audit log for employee registration and onboarding actions.
acceptance_criteria: Core employee registration and onboarding business rules are implemented for Corporate Expense Management
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for employee registration and onboarding
API/service behavior for employee registration and onboarding is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Employee Registration and Onboarding Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for employee registration and onboarding with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The employee registration and onboarding user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for employee registration and onboarding behave correctly
The employee registration and onboarding UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Harden Employee Registration and Onboarding Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to employee registration and onboarding. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for employee registration and onboarding are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in employee registration and onboarding are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for employee registration and onboarding passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Employee Registration and Onboarding Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for employee registration and onboarding. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for employee registration and onboarding are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for employee registration and onboarding are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical employee registration and onboarding scenarios
